description: BPC-157 is a synthetic pentadecapeptide with the sequence GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, corresponding to a fragment of a protection protein originally isolated from human gastric juice. It has the empirical formula C62H98N16O22 and a molecular weight of approximately 1419.5 Da. The peptide is proline-rich and classified as a cytoprotective reference compound in preclinical literature. BPC-157 has been used as a reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo studies of endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) signalling, angiogenic responses, and Src–Caveolin‑1–eNOS pathway modulation in vascular tissues. It is also employed in cell-based assays exploring cytoprotection and stress-response pathways in gastrointestinal and musculoskeletal models. Supplied as an HPLC-verified lyophilized powder at >98% purity for research use. Typically reconstituted in sterile water or buffered aqueous media for in‑vitro applications, and stored lyophilized at 2–8°C, with aliquots maintained at −20°C after reconstitution, protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: BPC-157 Reference Standard | Cytoprotective Peptide
seo_description: Synthetic pentadecapeptide (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV) reference standard for in-vitro cytoprotective and angiogenic signalling research. >98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Thymosin beta-4 (TB-500) is a highly conserved 43-amino-acid peptide originally isolated from calf thymus and encoded in humans by the TMSB4X gene. It has the sequence Ac-SDKPDMAEIEKFDKSKLKKTETQEKNPLPSKETIEQEKQAGES and a molecular weight of approximately 4963 Da. Thymosin beta-4 is an actin-sequestering peptide and a prototypical member of the beta-thymosin family. In research, TB-500 is used as a reference peptide in in-vitro and in-vivo studies of actin polymerization, cell migration, and cytoskeletal dynamics, and in models of connective tissue matrix deposition. It has been studied in experimental wound-chamber systems and other preclinical models investigating extracellular-matrix gene expression and tissue remodelling pathways. The peptide is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with typical research-grade purity ≥98% (HPLC). Reconstitution in sterile water or buffered aqueous solutions is commonly used for in-vitro assays, with lyophilized material stored at −20°C and protected from light and moisture. Reference standard for in-vitro research on actin dynamics and thymosin-family signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) Reference | Thymic Peptide
seo_description: Thymosin beta-4 reference peptide for in-vitro studies of actin dynamics and connective tissue pathways. ≥98% HPLC. Lyophilized. RUO.

description: Semaglutide is a synthetic peptide analog of human glucagon-like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) (7–37) in which Ala8 is replaced by α‑aminobutyric acid, Lys26 is acylated with a C18 diacid-containing spacer, and Lys34 is substituted with Arg. It has the empirical formula C187H291N45O59 and a molecular weight around 4113 Da in its peptide form. These structural modifications confer high affinity for serum albumin and increased resistance to DPP‑4-mediated degradation while retaining GLP‑1 receptor agonist pharmacology. Semaglutide is widely used as a reference GLP‑1 receptor agonist in in‑vitro studies of class B GPCR signalling, including cAMP accumulation, β‑arrestin recruitment, and receptor internalization in GLP‑1R‑expressing cell lines. It has also been employed in ex‑vivo systems examining pancreatic and cardiovascular GLP‑1R signalling cascades. Research-grade semaglutide is typically supplied as a lyophilized peptide with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is commonly reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer for biochemical and cell-based assays, and stored lyophilized at −20°C with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GLP‑1 receptor signalling and incretin-pathway pharmacology. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Semaglutide Reference Standard | GLP-1 Analog
seo_description: Modified GLP-1 analog reference standard for in-vitro GLP-1 receptor signalling studies. Lyophilized peptide, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Tirzepatide is a synthetic linear polypeptide incretin mimetic that functions as a dual agonist at the glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) receptor and GLP‑1 receptor. It is a 39-amino-acid peptide incorporating a C20 fatty-diacidated lysine side chain to enhance albumin binding and extend circulation time. The peptide has an approximate molecular weight of 4813 Da and is registered in PubChem under CID 166567236. As a dual GIP/GLP‑1 receptor ligand, tirzepatide is used in in‑vitro assays probing class B GPCR pharmacology, including cAMP signalling, β‑arrestin recruitment, and biased agonism at GIPR and GLP‑1R in recombinant and native cell systems. It is also applied as a reference compound in mechanistic studies of incretin receptor co‑agonism and receptor trafficking. Research formulations are supplied as lyophilized peptide with typical purity ≥98–99% by HPLC. For in‑vitro work, tirzepatide is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer, with lyophilized vials stored at −20°C and protected from light; reconstituted aliquots are kept frozen to maintain integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on dual GIP and GLP‑1 receptor signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Tirzepatide Reference Standard | Dual Incretin Peptide
seo_description: Dual GIP/GLP‑1 receptor agonist peptide reference for in-vitro incretin signalling research. Lyophilized, high-purity. RUO.

description: GHK‑Cu is a copper(II) complex of the endogenous tripeptide glycyl‑L‑histidyl‑L‑lysine, forming a 1:1 peptide–metal chelate. The peptide ligand (GHK) has the empirical formula C14H24N6O4, while the copper complex is reported with molecular weight around 400.9–744.3 g/mol depending on counterions and hydration state. GHK displays high affinity for Cu(II), coordinating via the histidine imidazole and terminal amine groups. GHK‑Cu is widely used as a reference copper tripeptide in in‑vitro and in‑vivo models examining extracellular-matrix gene expression, connective-tissue remodelling, and metalloproteinase–inhibitor balance. It has also been employed in transport and penetration studies across model stratum corneum membranes, and in cell systems exploring copper trafficking and redox-related signalling. Research-grade GHK‑Cu is supplied as a lyophilized powder with typical purity ≥98% by HPLC. It is readily soluble in water and aqueous buffers, forming blue solutions characteristic of Cu(II) complexes, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture to limit oxidation. Reference standard for in-vitro research on copper-peptide signalling and extracellular-matrix regulation pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: GHK-Cu Reference Standard | Copper Tripeptide
seo_description: Copper tripeptide (glycyl-histidyl-lysine Cu(II)) reference standard for in-vitro copper signalling and matrix-pathway research. Lyophilized. RUO.

description: MOTS‑c (mitochondrial open reading frame of the 12S rRNA‑c) is a 16‑amino‑acid peptide encoded by a short open reading frame within the mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene. The sequence is MRWQEMGYIFYPRKLR, and the peptide is synthesized in the cytoplasm using the nuclear genetic code before localizing to mitochondria under basal conditions. MOTS‑c is classified as a mitochondrial-derived regulatory peptide with reported interactions with metabolic pathways in preclinical models. In research, MOTS‑c is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro studies of cellular energy-sensing networks, including assays examining AMPK activation, folate cycle modulation, and downstream gene-expression programs under metabolic stress. It is also applied in cell and animal models exploring mitochondrial–nuclear communication and stress‑response signalling. Research-grade MOTS‑c is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at high purity (≥98% HPLC) for experimental use. It is typically reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer immediately prior to use, with lyophilized vials stored at −20°C and reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from repeated freeze–thaw. Reference standard for in-vitro research on mitochondrial-derived peptide signalling and AMPK-associated pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: MOTS-c Reference Standard | Mitochondrial Peptide
seo_description: 16-residue mitochondrial-derived peptide (MRWQEMGYIFYPRKLR) for in-vitro studies of AMPK and metabolic stress signalling. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Epithalon (Epitalon, AEDG) is a synthetic tetrapeptide with the sequence Ala‑Glu‑Asp‑Gly, originally designed based on the amino-acid composition of epithalamin, an extract from bovine pineal gland. It has the molecular formula C14H22N4O9 and a molecular weight of approximately 390.35 Da. Epithalon is classified as a pineal regulatory peptide in the short-peptide literature. Epithalon is employed as a reference compound in in‑vitro and animal studies investigating telomere biology, chromatin regulation, and pineal‑associated signalling pathways. It has been studied in cellular models for its effects on telomerase activity and gene-expression patterns associated with stress resistance and circadian regulation. Research-grade Epithalon is typically supplied as a lyophilized powder with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is readily soluble in water and aqueous buffers, and lyophilized material is stored at −20°C with reconstituted aliquots maintained frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on pineal peptide signalling and telomere-associated pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Epithalon Reference Standard | Pineal Tetrapeptide
seo_description: Synthetic tetrapeptide (Ala-Glu-Asp-Gly) reference standard for in-vitro research on pineal peptides and telomere-associated pathways. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Sermorelin is a synthetic peptide corresponding to the 1–29 N‑terminal fragment of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH), amidated at the C‑terminus. It comprises 29 amino acids with molecular formula approximately C149H246N44O42S and molecular weight about 3357–3418 Da depending on salt form. This fragment retains the full GHRH receptor agonist activity of the native hormone. Sermorelin is widely used as a reference GHRH analog in in‑vitro pituitary and heterologous expression systems to study class B GHRH receptor pharmacology. It is applied in assays of receptor binding, cAMP accumulation, and growth-hormone secretagogue signalling pathways in somatotroph cell models and transfected cell lines. Research-grade sermorelin is supplied predominantly as the acetate salt in lyophilized form with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute aqueous buffers and is stored lyophilized at −20°C, with reconstituted solutions kept frozen and protected from light to maintain peptide integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and growth hormone secretagogue pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Sermorelin Reference Standard | GHRH (1-29) Analog
seo_description: GHRH (1–29)-amide reference peptide for in-vitro GHRH receptor and growth hormone secretagogue signalling research. Lyophilized, high-purity. RUO.

description: CJC-1295 with DAC is a synthetic analog of human growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) (1–29) that incorporates a drug affinity complex (DAC) moiety to enable covalent binding to serum albumin. The peptide backbone is a modified GHRH(1–29) sequence (e.g., Tyr‑D‑Ala‑Asp‑Ala‑Ile‑Phe‑Thr‑Gln‑Ser‑Tyr‑Arg‑Lys‑Val‑Leu‑Ala‑Gln‑Leu‑Ser‑Ala‑Arg‑Lys‑Leu‑…) with empirical formula C165H269N47O46 reported for the DAC-conjugated form. The DAC group is typically attached via a lysine side chain to form a stable albumin-reactive entity. CJC-1295 DAC is used as a long-acting GHRH analog in in‑vitro and in‑vivo models examining class B GHRH receptor pharmacology and growth hormone secretagogue signalling. It serves as a reference compound in studies of receptor binding, cAMP generation, and pharmacokinetic extension via albumin bioconjugation. Research-grade CJC-1295 DAC is supplied as a lyophilized peptide at ≥98% purity by HPLC, typically as an acetate or trifluoroacetate salt. The material is reconstituted in sterile water or aqueous buffer immediately prior to use and stored lyophilized at −20°C, with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen and protected from light. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and albumin bioconjugation strategies. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: CJC-1295 DAC Reference Standard | GHRH Analog
seo_description: Long-acting GHRH(1–29) analog with DAC for in-vitro studies of growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Ipamorelin is a synthetic pentapeptide with the sequence Aib‑His‑D‑2‑Nal‑D‑Phe‑Lys‑NH2, where Aib is α‑aminoisobutyric acid and 2‑Nal is 3‑(2‑naphthyl)‑D‑alanine. It has the empirical formula C38H49N9O5 and a molecular weight of approximately 711.85 Da. Ipamorelin is a highly selective agonist of the ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR‑1a) and belongs to the class of non‑endogenous growth hormone secretagogues. In research, ipamorelin is widely used as a reference GHSR agonist in in‑vitro assays and ex‑vivo pituitary or hypothalamic systems. It is applied in binding, calcium-flux, and cAMP assays, as well as in perfused organ and animal models studying growth hormone secretagogue receptor signalling. Research-grade ipamorelin is provided mainly as the acetate salt in lyophilized form with purity ≥98% by HPLC. It shows good stability when stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, and is typically reconstituted in water, dilute acids, or mixed aqueous–organic solvents for experimental use. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ghrelin/growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Ipamorelin Reference Standard | GHSR Peptide
seo_description: Pentapeptide GHSR agonist (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) for in-vitro growth hormone secretagogue receptor research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: 5-Amino-1MQ is a synthetic small-molecule research probe belonging to the quaternary quinolinium class. It is commonly referenced by the cation name 5-amino-1-methylquinolinium (C10H11N2+) and is frequently supplied as an iodide salt (C10H11IN2) with CAS number 42464-96-0. The compound has been discussed in preclinical literature as a substrate-site–targeting inhibitor candidate for nicotinamide N‑methyltransferase (NNMT). In vitro, 5-Amino-1MQ is used as a reference NNMT-modulating small molecule in enzymatic assays, metabolite profiling workflows, and pathway studies assessing methylation-related biochemical readouts. It is applied in cell-free and cell-based systems interrogating NNMT activity and downstream metabolite changes in controlled experimental settings. Research-grade 5-Amino-1MQ iodide is typically supplied as a solid powder with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in polar solvents such as water and DMSO, and stability data and catalog guidance recommend storage at −20°C in a dry environment to preserve integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on NNMT-associated biochemical pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: 5-Amino-1MQ Reference Standard | NNMT Probe
seo_description: Quinolinium NNMT research probe (5-Amino-1-methylquinolinium iodide) for in-vitro nicotinamide N-methyltransferase pathway studies. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: NAD+ (nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide, oxidized form) is a ubiquitous pyridine nucleotide cofactor composed of an adenosine diphosphate ribose moiety linked via pyrophosphate to nicotinamide riboside. The oxidized species has empirical formula C21H26N7O14P2− (for the anionic form) and a molecular weight around 662.4 g/mol. NAD+ participates in redox reactions as an electron acceptor and is central to dehydrogenase-catalyzed metabolic transformations in biochemical systems. In research, NAD+ is used as a reference cofactor in enzyme-kinetic assays, mitochondrial function studies, and in‑vitro models of energy metabolism. It is routinely employed in spectrophotometric dehydrogenase assays, sirtuin activity measurements, and other in‑vitro systems that require defined pyridine nucleotide pools. Research-grade NAD+ is typically supplied as the disodium or free acid salt in lyophilized powder form with ≥98% purity. It is highly water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C in sealed containers protected from moisture and repeated freeze–thaw; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen depending on experimental timelines. Reference standard for in-vitro research on redox biochemistry and pyridine-nucleotide–dependent metabolic pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: NAD+ Reference Standard | Pyridine Nucleotide
seo_description: Oxidized nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide cofactor for in-vitro redox enzyme and metabolic pathway studies. Lyophilized, ≥98% purity. RUO.

description: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide analogue of the adrenocorticotropic hormone fragment ACTH(4–10) with the sequence Met‑Glu‑His‑Phe‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (MEHFPGP). It preserves the core ACTH(4–7) sequence and incorporates an additional Pro‑Gly‑Pro motif at the C‑terminus. The peptide has been characterized as a regulatory neuropeptide analog with high affinity for copper(II) ions in solution. Semax is used as a reference ACTH-derived peptide in in‑vitro and ex‑vivo models investigating neuropeptide signalling, neurotrophin expression, and metal-ion–associated oxidative processes. It has been studied in cell cultures for its interactions with copper(II) and for effects on viability under metal-induced stress conditions. Research-grade semax is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt at ≥98% HPLC purity for experimental work. It is water-soluble and generally stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted solutions maintained at low temperature and used promptly. Reference standard for in-vitro research on ACTH-fragment neuropeptides, metal–peptide interactions, and neurotrophin-related pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Semax Reference Standard | ACTH Fragment Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide ACTH(4–10) analog (MEHFPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and metal–peptide signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Selank is a synthetic heptapeptide with the sequence Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg‑Pro‑Gly‑Pro (TKPRPGP), designed as an analog of the endogenous tetrapeptide tuftsin (Thr‑Lys‑Pro‑Arg). The C‑terminal Pro‑Gly‑Pro extension enhances peptide stability while preserving the tuftsin motif. Selank is classified as a regulatory neuropeptide analog in the Russian peptide literature. Selank is used as a reference peptide in in‑vitro and animal models assessing neuropeptide signalling, synaptic plasticity markers, and neurotrophin expression. It has been examined in systems measuring brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) expression and in receptor-binding or gene-expression assays relevant to stress-response pathways. Research-grade Selank is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with typical purity ≥98% by HPLC. It is soluble in water and aqueous buffers and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen according to experimental schedules. Reference standard for in-vitro research on tuftsin-related neuropeptides and BDNF-associated signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Selank Reference Standard | Tuftsin Analog Peptide
seo_description: Heptapeptide tuftsin analog (TKPRPGP) for in-vitro neuropeptide and BDNF-pathway research. Lyophilized acetate salt, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Kisspeptin-10 (KP‑10) is a decapeptide fragment of the human KISS1 gene product, corresponding to residues 45–54 (Metastin 45–54) with the sequence Tyr‑Asn‑Trp‑Asn‑Ser‑Phe‑Gly‑Leu‑Arg‑Phe‑NH2 (YNWNSFGLRF‑NH2). It has the empirical formula C63H83N17O14 and a molecular weight of approximately 1302.4 g/mol. Kisspeptin‑10 is a high-affinity ligand for the G protein–coupled receptor KISS1R (GPR54), which plays a key role in the hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis. In vitro, kisspeptin‑10 is widely used as a reference peptide in receptor-binding, calcium-mobilization, and cAMP signalling assays in KISS1R‑expressing cell systems. It is also applied in pituitary and hypothalamic models exploring GnRH neuron activation and downstream gonadotropin-related pathways. Research-grade kisspeptin‑10 is supplied as a lyophilized acetate or trifluoroacetate salt with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute acetic acid, and is stored at −20°C protected from light; once reconstituted, aliquots are typically frozen to maintain peptide stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on KISS1R signalling and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal axis regulation. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Kisspeptin-10 Reference Standard | KISS1R Peptide
seo_description: Decapeptide KISS1R ligand (YNWNSFGLRF-NH2) for in-vitro GnRH neuron and hypothalamic–pituitary–gonadal signalling research. ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Oxytocin is a cyclic nonapeptide hormone and neuropeptide with the sequence Cys‑Tyr‑Ile‑Gln‑Asn‑Cys‑Pro‑Leu‑Gly‑NH2 (CYIQNCPLG‑NH2), featuring a disulfide bridge between Cys1 and Cys6. It has the empirical formula C43H66N12O12S2 and is classified as a member of the vasopressin/oxytocin peptide family. Oxytocin acts via the oxytocin receptor (OXTR), a class A GPCR. In research, oxytocin is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding and functional assays investigating OXTR-mediated G‑protein signalling, intracellular calcium flux, and β‑arrestin recruitment. It is also applied in myometrial strip preparations, neuronal cultures, and ex‑vivo tissue systems to characterize oxytocin receptor pharmacology and downstream signalling cascades. Research-grade oxytocin is supplied as a lyophilized peptide, often as an acetate salt, with ≥98% HPLC purity. It is readily soluble in water and dilute acids, and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are typically kept refrigerated or frozen and used within defined stability windows. Reference standard for in-vitro research on oxytocin receptor signalling and vasopressin/oxytocin-family neuropeptides. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Oxytocin Reference Standard | Neuropeptide Hormone
seo_description: Cyclic nonapeptide hormone (CYIQNCPLG-NH2) for in-vitro oxytocin receptor and vasopressin-family signalling research. Lyophilized, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Bacteriostatic Water is sterile, non-pyrogenic water preserved with 0.9% (w/v) benzyl alcohol as a bacteriostatic agent. It is supplied in multi-use vials for laboratory reconstitution of lyophilized research peptides and reference compounds. The benzyl alcohol preservative inhibits microbial growth in multi-use vials, allowing repeat access over defined storage periods in research protocols. Bacteriostatic Water is used as a reconstitution solvent in in-vitro and ex-vivo research workflows requiring sterile aqueous diluent with defined composition. It is not a therapeutic product and is not characterized for human or animal administration. Supplied as a sterile clear colorless solution in sealed multi-use vials. Store at room temperature (15-25°C) away from direct light. Once entered, discard within 28 days of first use per laboratory best practice for preserved sterile solutions. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Bacteriostatic Water for Research | Reconstitution Supply

description: The BPC-157 + TB-500 blend is a multi-peptide research preparation combining the synthetic gastric pentadecapeptide BPC-157 (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV, ~1419.5 Da) with the 43-amino-acid thymic peptide Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500, ~4963 Da). BPC-157 is classified as a cytoprotective peptide fragment derived from a protein isolated from human gastric juice, whereas Thymosin Beta-4 is an actin-sequestering beta-thymosin family peptide encoded by the TMSB4X gene. Commercial research vials typically contain equal mass fractions of BPC-157 and TB-500 (for example, 5 mg of each peptide in a 10 mg lyophilized blend), corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this mass ratio translates to an approximate molar ratio of ~3.5:1 for BPC-157:TB-500. The blend is used as a multi-component reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models where cytoprotective and actin-associated peptide pathways are studied in parallel. The BPC-157 + TB-500 blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of high HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution in aqueous media for analytical and cell-based assays. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted aliquots maintained at low temperature to preserve peptide integrity. Reference standard for in-vitro research on cytoprotective and thymosin-family signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

description: The Glow Blend is a three-component research preparation containing the copper tripeptide complex GHK-Cu (glycyl-L-histidyl-L-lysine–Cu(II)), the gastric pentadecapeptide BPC-157 (GEPPPGKPADDAGLV), and the 43-residue thymic peptide Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500). GHK-Cu is a high-affinity Cu(II) chelate of the endogenous tripeptide GHK, whereas BPC-157 and TB-500 represent cytoprotective and actin-binding peptide classes, respectively. Commercial Glow Blend vials are often formulated at a 5:1:1 mass ratio of GHK-Cu:BPC-157:TB-500; for example, 50 mg GHK-Cu, 10 mg BPC-157, and 10 mg TB-500 in a 70 mg vial. When molecular weights are taken into account (GHK-Cu ~400–744 Da, BPC-157 ~1419.5 Da, Thymosin Beta-4 ~4963 Da), this 5:1:1 mass ratio corresponds approximately to a molar ratio on the order of ~62:3.5:1 (GHK-Cu:BPC-157:TB-500). The blend is used as a multi-peptide reference standard in in-vitro models where copper-peptide signalling, cytoprotective pathways, and thymosin-family mechanisms are studied concurrently. The Glow Blend is supplied as a lyophilized, water-soluble powder with each component manufactured to high HPLC purity suitable for analytical and cell-based work. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C, protected from light and moisture, and reconstituted with aqueous media immediately prior to use; aliquots are typically maintained frozen for short-term use. Reference standard for in-vitro research on copper tripeptides, cytoprotective peptides, and thymosin-family signalling pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

description: The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is a two-component reference preparation containing CJC-1295 NO DAC (also termed Mod GRF(1–29)), a 29-residue GHRH analog, together with Ipamorelin, a synthetic pentapeptide growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR-1a) agonist. CJC-1295 NO DAC has a reported molecular weight around 3367.9 Da, while Ipamorelin (Aib-His-D-2-Nal-D-Phe-Lys-NH2) has a molecular weight of approximately 711.8 Da. Typical research vials contain 10 mg total peptide comprising 5 mg CJC-1295 NO DAC and 5 mg Ipamorelin, corresponding to a 1:1 mass ratio. When normalized for molecular weight, this equates to an approximate molar ratio of ~1:4.8 (CJC-1295:Ipamorelin), reflecting the lower molecular weight and higher molar concentration of Ipamorelin at equal mass. The blend is used as a dual-peptide reference standard in in-vitro and ex-vivo models investigating GHRH receptor and GHSR-1a signalling in parallel. The CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Blend is supplied as a lyophilized powder of ≥99% HPLC purity suitable for reconstitution in aqueous media. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots frozen or refrigerated as appropriate for the experimental schedule. Reference standard for in-vitro research on growth hormone-releasing hormone and growth hormone secretagogue receptor pathways. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin Reference Blend | Dual Secretagogue
seo_description: Dual-peptide blend (CJC-1295 NO DAC + Ipamorelin) at ~1:4.8 molar ratio for in-vitro GHRH and GHSR-1a signalling research. ≥99% HPLC. RUO.

description: Retatrutide (LY3437943) is a synthetic 39-amino-acid polypeptide based on a glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide (GIP) backbone, incorporating non-coded residues (such as Aib at select positions) and a C20 fatty diacid side chain to enhance albumin binding. It has the molecular formula C221H342N46O68 and a reported molecular weight of approximately 4731.3 Da. Retatrutide is classified as a multi-receptor incretin-family peptide. In functional in-vitro assays, retatrutide acts as a potent full agonist at three class B G protein–coupled receptors: the glucagon receptor (GCGR), the GIP receptor (GIPR), and the GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R). cAMP accumulation studies in human receptor-expressing cell lines report EC50 values of 5.79 nM at GCGR, 0.064 nM at GIPR, and 0.775 nM at GLP-1R, with corresponding nanomolar binding affinities in radioligand-displacement experiments. Research-grade retatrutide is supplied as a lyophilized acetate or related salt at high HPLC purity for biochemical and cell-based studies. It is typically reconstituted in aqueous buffer or DMSO for in-vitro work and stored lyophilized at −20°C protected from light, with reconstituted aliquots kept frozen to maintain stability. Reference standard for in-vitro research on triple agonism at GLP-1R, GIPR, and glucagon receptors and incretin-pathway signalling. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.

description: PT-141 (Bremelanotide) is a synthetic cyclic heptapeptide analog of the endogenous melanocortin peptide α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH). It is structurally described as Ac-Nle-cyclo(Asp-His-D-Phe-Arg-Trp-Lys)-OH and has the empirical formula C50H68N14O10 with a molecular weight of approximately 1025.16 Da. The lactam bridge between the Asp and Lys side chains constrains the peptide into a cyclized conformation characteristic of melanocortin analogs. PT-141 is studied as a non-selective melanocortin receptor agonist with prominent activity at MC3R and MC4R, both G protein–coupled receptors expressed in central and peripheral tissues. In vitro, it is used as a reference ligand in receptor-binding assays, cAMP accumulation studies, and signalling-bias experiments across melanocortin receptor subtypes. Research-grade PT-141 is commonly supplied as a lyophilized acetate or free-base peptide of ≥97–98% HPLC purity. It is soluble in water and dilute acids and is stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture, with reconstituted solutions maintained at low temperature. Reference standard for in-vitro research on melanocortin receptor signalling, especially MC3R and MC4R pharmacology. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: PT-141 (Bremelanotide) Reference | Melanocortin Agonist
seo_description: Cyclic α-MSH analog for in-vitro melanocortin receptor (MC3R/MC4R) signalling research. Lyophilized peptide, ≥98% HPLC. RUO.

description: Tesamorelin is a synthetic 44-amino-acid analog of human growth hormone–releasing hormone (GHRH) with an N-terminal trans-3-hexenoyl modification and a Tyr1 substitution, formally designated N-(trans-3-hexenoyl)-[Tyr1]hGHRH(1–44)NH2. It closely mirrors endogenous GHRH sequence while incorporating modifications that enhance peptide stability and pharmacokinetic properties. Tesamorelin has the molecular formula C221H366N72O67S and a molecular weight around 5135.9 Da. Tesamorelin is a selective agonist of the GHRH receptor (GHRHR), a class B G protein–coupled receptor expressed on pituitary somatotrophs. In vitro, it is used as a reference GHRH analog in binding, cAMP accumulation, and downstream signalling assays in GHRHR-expressing cell lines and pituitary cell models, enabling detailed characterization of GHRH receptor pharmacology. Research-grade tesamorelin is supplied as a lyophilized acetate salt with high HPLC purity, intended for reconstitution in aqueous buffer or sterile water immediately before use. Lyophilized vials are stored at −20°C protected from light and moisture; reconstituted solutions are kept refrigerated or frozen as appropriate for experimental timelines. Reference standard for in-vitro research on GHRH receptor signalling and growth hormone axis regulation. For Research Use Only. Not for human consumption. Not intended for therapeutic, diagnostic, or clinical use.
seo_title: Tesamorelin Reference Standard | GHRH Analog Peptide
